review.agent_angle: WordPress RTL Integration Specialist: Focused on ensuring the HTML snippet survives WordPress content filtering, maintains strict RTL layout against theme overrides, and handles external assets correctly without relying on document-level tags.
wordpress_critical_checks: Strip <html>, <head>, and <body> tags before inserting into Gutenberg 'Custom HTML' block; WordPress provides these.
Convert the Google Fonts <link> tags to a @import rule inside a <style> tag within the snippet, as <link> tags in the body are often stripped or cause validation errors.
Verify that the WordPress theme does not force LTR direction or strip the dir='rtl' attribute from the wrapper elements.
Ensure the external Cloudfront image URL is accessible and not blocked by the host's Content Security Policy or hotlinking protections.
html_improvement_notes: Add a <style> block at the very beginning of the snippet: <style>@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Heebo:wght@400;500;600;700;800&display=swap');</style>.
Use !important on critical inline styles like direction: rtl, font-family, and text-align to prevent aggressive WordPress theme overrides.
The provided source is truncated at 'של החיישן במער'; ensure the full HTML content is generated.
Add specific list-style positioning for RTL (e.g., list-style-position: outside; padding-right: 20px;) as WordPress themes often reset list styles.
n8n_workflow_notes: Configure the HTML generation node to output only the <article>...</article> block, excluding the <!DOCTYPE>, <html>, <head>, and <body> wrappers.
Implement a string replacement step in the workflow to automatically convert <link> href tags to <style>@import url(...);</style> for WordPress compatibility.
Add a validation check at the end of the workflow to ensure no document-level tags remain in the final payload.
prompt_notes: Request the full, untruncated source text for the article.
Explicitly instruct the model to output a WordPress-compatible HTML snippet (no <html>, <head>, or <body> tags).
Emphasize that all styling must be inline or contained within an internal <style> block to ensure 1:1 rendering independent of the theme.
qa_acceptance: Template pastes into a Gutenberg 'Custom HTML' block without triggering block recovery errors.
The 'Heebo' font loads and applies correctly on the frontend.
The layout remains strictly RTL and does not flip or misalign when viewed on the WordPress site.
The external logo image renders without broken image icons.
The <details> element functions correctly for the table of contents without CSS conflicts.
